_comment: Seed data for the readiness checklist, aligned to the OTCID Basic Market regime (effective July 1, 2025 — successor to Pink Current). Loaded into the app database on first boot; descriptive fields refresh on every boot, status/owner/notes are preserved. status: todo | in_progress | blocked | done | na

phases.name: Formation
phases.goal: Legally existing Delaware C corp with clean records

phases.name: Capitalization & Records
phases.goal: Shares issued with airtight paper trail; GAAP financials

phases.name: Public Company Infrastructure
phases.goal: Transfer agent, CUSIP, counsel, accountant engaged

phases.name: OTCID Application & Form 211
phases.goal: Quoted on the OTCID Basic Market

phases.name: Ongoing Compliance
phases.goal: Stay current every quarter and year; avoid Pink Limited / Expert Market demotion

requirements.title: Choose company name and check availability
requirements.description: Delaware name search + trademark screen. Record final name in the Company Master.

requirements.title: Engage Delaware registered agent
requirements.description: Commercial registered agent required (~$50-300/yr). Record in Company Master.

requirements.title: File Certificate of Incorporation
requirements.description: Public-company-ready charter: high authorized common (100M-500M typical for OTC issuers), blank-check preferred (10-20M), $0.0001 par (keeps DE filing tax at the ~$89 minimum), 102(b)(7) exculpation + indemnification. Generate from template, counsel review, file with DE Division of Corporations.

requirements.title: Action of Sole Incorporator
requirements.description: Incorporator adopts charter, elects the initial board, and resigns.

requirements.title: Initial board consent (organizational)
requirements.description: Adopt bylaws, appoint officers, set fiscal year, authorize founder share issuance (uncertificated book-entry per DGCL §158), bank account, EIN, service providers.

requirements.title: Adopt bylaws
requirements.description: Board-adopted bylaws with stockholder written consent (DGCL §228), indemnification, uncertificated shares, annual meeting mechanics (DGCL §211: compellable after 13 months without one).

requirements.title: Obtain EIN
requirements.description: IRS online / Form SS-4 (free). Record EIN in Company Master.

requirements.title: Open corporate bank account
requirements.description: Requires filed charter + EIN + board consent. Every share-consideration payment must flow through this account with records kept.

requirements.title: Foreign qualification (if operating outside DE)
requirements.description: Qualify in each state with offices/employees (note: CA adds $800/yr minimum franchise tax).

requirements.title: Issue founder shares with executed purchase agreements
requirements.description: Board consent + stock purchase agreement + proof of consideration (wire/check copy) for EVERY issuance. File 83(b) elections within 30 days if shares vest — IRS Form 15620 can now be filed online.

requirements.title: Maintain stock ledger / issuance history
requirements.description: Every change in shares outstanding recorded in the Company Master issuance_history; the ledger regenerates from it and must reconcile to outstanding shares at all times. FINRA traces every issuance since inception — gaps are the #1 cause of Form 211 deficiency letters.

requirements.title: Build shareholder base via exempt offerings
requirements.description: Reg D 506(b)/(c) or 4(a)(2) placements (file Form D — needs free EDGAR CIK codes). Practitioner benchmarks for Form 211: ~25+ non-affiliate holders who PAID CASH ≥12 months before filing, mostly round lots; ≥1M shares outstanding with ≥250K free-trading. Rule 144: 12-month hold for non-reporting issuers.

requirements.title: Bad-actor checks (Rule 506(d))
requirements.description: Questionnaires for all officers, directors, 20%+ holders, and promoters before any Reg D offering.

requirements.title: GAAP financial statements
requirements.description: Balance sheet, income statement, cash flows, changes in stockholders' equity + notes, presented comparatively. Two fiscal years (or since inception) + interims. Audit NOT required for OTCID, but preparer and qualifications must be disclosed; statements must be machine-readable.

requirements.title: Accounting system & records
requirements.description: Bookkeeping from day one. Every share-related transaction must trace to bank records.

requirements.title: Engage securities counsel
requirements.description: Guides 15c2-11/Form 211, exemption compliance, and OTCID disclosure; is named in the Management Certification. Record in Company Master.

requirements.title: Engage SEC-registered transfer agent (Verified Shares Program)
requirements.description: OTCID REQUIRES a transfer agent participating in the Transfer Agent Verified Shares Program that feeds share data to OTC Markets. Common OTC agents: Colonial, VStock, Pacific, Securities Transfer Corp, Action, Equiniti. Setup typically $2,500-5,000 + monthly.

requirements.title: Obtain CUSIP number
requirements.description: Apply at cusip.com/apply with corporate documents. $210 first identifier, 1-2 business days. Needed before Form 211/DTC.

requirements.title: Engage accountant
requirements.description: GAAP competence required; PCAOB registration only needed if targeting OTCQB/OTCQX later or electing audited status.

requirements.title: Plan DTC eligibility
requirements.description: Issuers can't apply directly — a DTC participant (broker-dealer/bank) sponsors the application with offering docs + tradability opinion + TA agreement. Without DTC eligibility, real trading is impractical. Start lining up the sponsor before quotation.

requirements.title: Open OTCIQ account & complete Verified Company Profile
requirements.description: Apply via OTC Markets Gateway; subscribe to the Disclosure & News Service; complete and verify the company profile (re-verify at least every 6 months thereafter).

requirements.title: Post initial disclosure statement + financials on OTCIQ
requirements.description: Per OTCID Disclosure Guidelines v5: cover page (shell/change-in-control checkboxes), items 1-10 including issuance history for 2 fiscal years + interim, convertible debt table, CEO AND CFO certifications, machine-readable financials in the same document.

requirements.title: Submit initial Management Certification
requirements.description: Officer-signed certification via OTCIQ (replaced the attorney letter July 1, 2025): reporting standard, obligations, counsel, officers/directors, 5%+ holders, convertible note holders, shell status.

requirements.title: Engage sponsoring market maker & assemble Form 211 package
requirements.description: A FINRA member market maker files Form 211 via FINRA Gateway (new streamlined platform since March 30, 2026). OTC Link ATS's Qualified-IDQS reviews serve OTCQX/OTCQB applicants — for OTCID, plan on a sponsoring market maker. FINRA Rule 5250 PROHIBITS paying the market maker for filing — costs flow through counsel. No official review SLA; ~30-45 days for clean filings, 60-90+ days for shells/reverse mergers.

requirements.title: Pay OTCID fees & receive symbol
requirements.description: Application fee $3,500 (one-time, paid via ACH/wire/card at Gateway submission); annual issuer fee $8,040/yr (verified against the fee schedule updated July 1, 2026 — raised from $7,500). Termination for non-payment: $1,000 re-activation within 30 days, reapplication after. Symbol assigned after FINRA processes the 211; quotation begins.

requirements.title: Quarterly report (Q1)
requirements.description: Disclosure statement + unaudited comparative GAAP interims via OTCIQ within 45 days of quarter end. Interims older than 6 months are stale.

requirements.title: Quarterly report (Q2)
requirements.description: Due within 45 days of quarter end.

requirements.title: Quarterly report (Q3)
requirements.description: Due within 45 days of quarter end.

requirements.title: Annual report
requirements.description: Disclosure statement + annual comparative GAAP financials within 90 days of fiscal year end. Keep financials younger than 16 months at all costs — staler means Expert Market.

requirements.title: Annual Management Certification
requirements.description: Via OTCIQ, concurrently with the annual report and no later than 45 days after the annual report deadline (≈ FYE + 135 days).

requirements.title: OTCIQ profile verification
requirements.description: Verify the company profile at least every 6 months, and immediately after any Change in Control.

requirements.title: Material news disclosure
requirements.description: Publish material events promptly through the OTC Disclosure & News Service; reflect them in the next periodic report. Officers/directors/affiliates must disclose affiliate status to their broker before trading.

requirements.title: Corporate action notices to FINRA (Rule 6490/10b-17)
requirements.description: Dividends, splits, name changes, mergers: notify FINRA at least 10 BUSINESS DAYS before the record date.

requirements.title: Late filing protocol
requirements.description: If any report will be late: file a Notification of Late Filing within 1 business day of the due date — extends quarterly +5 calendar days, annual +15 calendar days. Missing eligibility means automatic removal to Pink Limited (or Expert Market if financials are stale >16 months); requalification is automatic once cured (absent a Change in Control).

requirements.title: Annual stockholder action & DE franchise tax
requirements.description: Elect directors annually (meeting or DGCL §228 written consent). DE annual report + franchise tax due March 1 — use the assumed par value method (with high authorized shares the authorized-shares method can hit the $200K cap; assumed-par typically lands near $400-800 when most shares are issued).

requirements.title: OTCID annual fee & good standing
requirements.description: Pay the OTC Markets annual fee; maintain DE good standing; respond to OTC Markets inquiries.

requirements.title: Blue sky monitoring for secondary trading
requirements.description: OTC Markets' statewide secondary-trading exemptions (41 states OTCQX / 37 OTCQB as of Sept 2025) generally do NOT cover OTCID — rely on manual exemptions (e.g., Mergent listing, available in ~44 jurisdictions) or state-by-state analysis with counsel.
